search_queries_used: site:college-optometrists.org red eye clinical management guideline
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Dose tables are outside this source-first documentation mapping.
Unopened summaries were not used for item-level claims.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adult and child conjunctivitis and selected keratitis warnings; not exact for every red-eye cause.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth red-eye assessment; partial for in-person corneal, pupil, pressure, or fundal examination.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; specialist pathways and in-person examination require clinician review.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 due for revision in 2029 and the College microbial-keratitis Version 14 were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer DHA conjunctivitis issue or College microbial-keratitis version was identified on the official pages.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped red-eye, discharge, itching, burning, grittiness, watering, laterality, exposure, and course fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped visual change, severe pain, photophobia, trauma, headache with nausea, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, and severe foreign-body warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped history questions and clinician-assessed visual acuity, conjunctiva, discharge, cornea, and pupil documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-led eye care, contact-lens, return, review, referral, and medication-plan documentation without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped contact-lens red-eye, pain, photophobia, discharge, blurred vision, and corneal concern fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports emergency-context review; no additional legacy items are mapped from this section.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Broad bloodshot or ocular-hyperaemia synonyms, eyelid and movement examination, fluorescein, red reflex, fundoscopy, visual fields, previous records, and exact medication choices remain unsupported.
Conjunctivitis, keratitis, glaucoma, or other diagnosis must remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
